rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component ports
Use implementation annexes only for implementation-level bindings like assignments
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Maintain the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections as the input AADL model
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the most specific valid scope to maintain architectural clarity
Preserve the original AADL structure during fusion to avoid unintended side effects
Ensure that all referenced identifiers in AGREE annex blocks are visible in the target scope
Avoid placing assumptions or guarantees in scopes where they cannot be validated
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` for AGREE contract blocks
Place component type contracts in component type annexes
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes
Use valid visible identifiers in AGREE expressions
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions or guarantees in implementation annexes unless they reference implementation-specific features
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Do not place AGREE annex blocks in scopes where the referenced identifiers are not visible
Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax for assume, guarantee, assign, and validator directives
